text:
Once upon a time, in a quiet town nestled among mountains, there lived a mysterious parrot. People didn't speak much, but they knew the parrot could tell stories of ancient forests far away. They liked those stories because they felt a bit like those legendary trees.

One chilly afternoon, a young girl named Lucy visited the park. She loved animals. As she walked along, listening to the parrot's melodious singing, she heard an odd tale about a lost kingdom.

"You see," the parrot began, crestfallen but cheerful, "I've heard tales of a magical forest I've never seen. Every morning, there's music there—I believe it's lost children singing. Every spring, birds arrive carrying treasures they can't fly with. Just one little leap."

"And you've sung about this?" Lucy asked.

"The same. Just last autumn, you listened. A bluebird slipped; we fluttered him skyward. Gifts came our way: a flute made of star
